Census Data Quality Report

Source: R/data_quality.R
census_data_quality.Rd

Analyzes census data for missing values and suppression patterns. When data includes suppression flag columns (created by get_census(..., preserve_suppression_flags = TRUE)), provides detailed breakdown by suppression type. Otherwise, reports NA counts.

Statistics Canada suppresses data for various reasons including:

  • x, X: Data suppressed for confidentiality

  • F: Too unreliable to be published (high coefficient of variation)

  • ..., ..: Not applicable or not available

  • -: Zero or rounded to zero

  • N: Not applicable for reference period

  • *, **: Various special cases

Usage

census_data_quality(data, warn_threshold = 0.2, quiet = FALSE)

Arguments

data

A data frame returned by get_census().

warn_threshold

Numeric between 0 and 1. If the missing rate exceeds this threshold for any variable, a warning is issued. Default is 0.2 (20%).

quiet

If TRUE, suppress warnings about high suppression rates. Default is FALSE.

Value

A tibble with data quality statistics for each census vector variable:

variable

The census vector code

total

Total number of observations

non_missing

Number of non-NA values

missing

Number of NA values

missing_pct

Percentage of missing values

x_count

Count of 'x'/'X' suppressions (if flag columns present)

F_count

Count of 'F' suppressions (if flag columns present)

other_na

Count of other NA types (if flag columns present)

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Basic usage - shows NA counts without breakdown
data <- get_census("CA21", regions = list(CMA = "59933"),
                   vectors = c("v_CA21_434"), level = "DA")
census_data_quality(data)

# With suppression flags preserved - shows detailed breakdown
data <- get_census("CA21", regions = list(CMA = "59933"),
                   vectors = c("v_CA21_434"), level = "DA",
                   preserve_suppression_flags = TRUE)
census_data_quality(data)
} # }
